summary: A compact two-story brick building with a gable roof, featuring a white concrete foundation and platform. The structure displays brick and stone walls with horizontal banding, multiple small windows on each floor, and a dark wood/dark oak roof with stepped gable design. The building sits on a raised white foundation platform.
elements: windows (approximately 4 per floor, small square panes)
rebuild_hints: Build 8x8 rectangular footprint with 2 floors of brick walls
Add horizontal banding using stonebrick or lighter brick between floor levels
Place small windows (1-2 block wide) symmetrically on each floor, approximately 4 windows per side
Construct gable roof using dark oak wood with stepped/tiered design on front and back
Create raised white concrete platform foundation extending 1-2 blocks around base
Ensure roof peak is centered and symmetrical
Use brick variants and stone brick for visual texture and detail
uncertainties: Exact window placement and count per floor cannot be precisely determined from all angles
Interior layout and room configuration not visible
Exact height measurement uncertain; estimate based on visible block proportions
Specific brick variant (red brick vs. other types) difficult to confirm
Whether foundation extends fully around building or only visible sides
Presence of doors not clearly visible in any view
